persona.situation: Language student with parents' support but insufficient own bank balance

valid_reason: The context on Page 23 explicitly states that the principle for financial proof is a bank balance certificate, but allows alternative documents if support is received from a government or enterprise. It also lists 'parents' financial ability' under preparation documents, implying that parental support is a valid category, but the answer correctly deduces that a notarized letter alone is insufficient without the accompanying proof of the parents' actual funds (bank statements), as the regulation requires proof of 'ability' (funds) rather than just a promise.

question: I'm a D-2 visa holder from Uzbekistan who left Korea for 3 months to visit family, missing my NHIS payments. I just returned and want to re-acquire eligibility immediately. I know I might need to pay the back-fees or wait 6 months, but since I have a D-2 visa, do I still have to pay the unpaid charges to get my certificate back? Also, I tried applying for online banking to deposit money and get interest, but the bank says I need an Official Certificate to log in. Can I get that certificate using just my phone, or do I physically have to visit a bank branch in Korea? Also, if I need to print my NHIS certificate urgently tomorrow, can I use the machine at the Namsan Community Service Center near Beomeosa Station without calling the center first?
answer: Let's break this down step-by-step based on your situation. First, regarding your NHIS eligibility: Since you are a D-2 visa holder, the rules explicitly state you are exempted from paying the unpaid insurance charges incurred while you were overseas. Therefore, you do not need to pay the back-fees or wait 6 months; you can regain eligibility immediately by contacting NHIS upon arrival. Second, for the bank login issue: The guidelines specify that an 'Official Certificate' is required to log in as a private user, and you must visit a bank in Korea individually to get this certificate issued; it cannot be done solely via a phone app if you don't already have it. Finally, for the NHIS certificate printing: The document mentions that Certificate Issuance Machines are located at Community Service Centers like the Namsan Center, but the process requires you to first call the NHIS center, select your language, and provide personal information to request the certificate before you can proceed with printing. So, you cannot just walk up and print it without the prior phone request. In summary: You don't pay back-fees, you must visit a bank for the login certificate, and you must call NHIS before using the machine.

(2) Korea’s Financial Institutions
Some foreign workers and multicultural family 
members prefer to keep their money at home 
instead of with a bank. But it is difficult to safe-
guard large sums of money at home and some-
times you may lose it. So, keeping your money 
at a bank or another well-known financial 
institution is a good personal banking practice 
as it is safe and can earn extra money (interest). 
So, what are the different types of financial in-
stitution in Korea? Financial institutions can be 
divided into banks and non-bank financial insti-
tutions.
Banks offer almost all kinds of consumer finan-
cial services related to money transactions such 
as depositing and withdrawing, lending, sending 
money abroad, exchanging one currency into 
another, issuing cards, and paying bills. As such, 
a bank is a financial institution that provides es-
sential financial services in Korea. 
Among the non-bank financial institutions, 
mutual savings banks, community credit coop-
eratives, credit union, agricultural cooperative 
(Nonghyup (NH)), fisheries cooperative (Suhyup) 
and Korea Post provide financial services similar 
to those of banks. You can use these financial in-
stitutions in a way almost similar to that of banks 
but they typically offer higher deposit and lend-
ing interest rates than banks.
Accordingly, you can choose one that is conve-
nient for you from among the variety of financial 
institutions in Korea. Nowadays, many financial 
transactions are conducted through internet 
banking or mobile banking; but when you open 
a new account or if you need assistance, it will be 
more advisable and convenient to visit a finan-
cial institution near your home. 
In addition, insurance companies, securities 
companies, and credit card companies are also 
the financial institutions that you can use rela-
tively frequently for financial services.
(2) 한국의 금융회사
외국인 근로자나 다문화 가족 가운데 금융회사
를 이용하지 않고 집에 돈을 보관하는 사람들이 
있는데, 금액이 클 경우 보관이 어렵고 돈을 잃
어버릴 수도 있습니다. 돈을 은행과 같은 금융회
사에 맡기면 안전할 뿐 아니라 맡긴 돈에 대한 
대가(이자)를 받을 수 있어 이득입니다.
그럼 한국에는 어떤 금융회사가 있을까요? 금융
회사의 종류는 크게 은행과 은행이 아닌 금융회
사로 나눕니다. 
은행에서는 입금(예금, 돈을 맡김), 출금(돈을 찾
음), 대출(돈을 빌림), 송금(돈을 보냄), 환전(다
른 나라 돈으로 바꿈), 카드 발급, 공과금 납부 
등 돈과 관련된 거의 모든 업무를 처리할 수 있
습니다. 이처럼 다양한 서비스를 이용하기 위해
서 한국 생활에서 은행은 반드시 이용해야 할 금
융회사입니다. 
한편 은행과 비슷한 업무를 하는 금융회사로 상
호저축은행, 새마을금고, 신용협동조합(신협), 
농업협동조합(농협), 수산업협동조합(수협), 우
체국 등이 있습니다. 이들 금융회사의 경우 이용
하는 방법은 은행과 거의 비슷하지만, 돈을 맡길 
때와 빌릴 때 모두 은행보다 이자율이 높은 편입
니다. 
이처럼 한국에는 다양한 금융회사가 있으므로 
자신이 편리하게 이용할 수 있는 금융회사를 선
택하면 됩니다. 요즘은 금융거래를 인터넷뱅킹
이나 모바일뱅킹으로 많이 하지만, 통장을 만들
거나 도움이 필요할 경우에는 집 근처에 있는 금
융회사를 이용하는 것이 편리할 것입니다. 
이 외에 금융생활을 하면서 비교적 자주 이용하
는 금융회사로는 보험회사, 증권회사, 신용카드
회사 등이 있습니다.
I. How to Use Banking Services
18

이런 실수를 대비하는 한 가지 방법은 ‘지연이체 
서비스’를 이용하는 것입니다. 이것은 송금 명령
을 실행한 후, 최소 3시간 후에 돈이 상대방 계
좌로 입금되도록 하는 서비스로, 돈이 이체되기 
전에 실수로 잘못 입력한 송금 명령을 취소할 수 
있습니다. 지연이체 서비스는 인터넷뱅킹 또는 
은행 영업점을 방문해 신청할 수 있습니다.
만약 다른 사람의 계좌로 이미 돈이 이체되었다
면 바로 은행에 연락해서 반환 신청을 합니다.

question: I'm a senior from Brazil planning to retire here after my PhD, but I'm worried about my long-term savings. I already contribute to the National Pension and have a company severance plan. If I decide to buy a personal pension insurance now to fill the gap, I'm scared that if an emergency happens and I have to cancel the policy before I retire, I'll lose all the money I paid in. Since the guide says the enrollment period is long and early termination means losing premiums, is there any specific strategy or type of plan I should look for to minimize this loss risk compared to just sticking with the mandatory public systems?
answer:
Based on the provided text, we must first deduce the nature of the risk you face. The guide explicitly states that pension insurance is designed for long-term retirement support, and a critical warning is that terminating the policy mid-term often results in not receiving a portion of the paid premiums back. This implies that the primary financial risk is liquidity loss due to the long lock-in period required for these products. 

Comparing this to your existing options: the National Pension and Severance Payment System are described as 'public' or mandatory systems. Typically, public systems have different statutory rules regarding refunds (often allowing for refunds of contributions under specific conditions like leaving the country, though text details are limited here), whereas the 'personal' pension insurance is a voluntary commercial product where the long duration is a core feature of the contract to ensure income stability later. 

The text does not offer a specific 'low-risk' product to avoid this loss, but it offers a logical inference on how to manage it: it advises selecting a plan that is 'affordable' relative to your income. The reasoning is that if the monthly payment is too high, you are more likely to be forced to cancel due to financial strain, triggering the penalty. Therefore, the strategy to minimize risk is not necessarily choosing a different 'type' of plan, but strictly selecting a payment amount you can sustain indefinitely without needing to withdraw. There is no mention of a 'surrender value' or partial refund guarantee for early exit in this specific text, so the only protective measure available is financial discipline in choosing a payment tier that matches your current and projected future income.

(3) [자동차보험] 자동차 사고로 인한 
피해를 보상하기 위한 보험 
 
자동차보험은 자동차 사고로 인한 인적, 물적 피
해에 대비하기 위한 보험입니다. 자동차보험은 
자동차를 구입하면 의무적으로 가입하는 책임보
험과 운전자가 임의로 가입할 수 있는 종합보험
이 있습니다. 
책임보험에 가입하면 사고를 냈을 때 피해를 입
은 상대방과 그 차량에 대해 한도 내에서 보상을 
받을 수 있습니다. 만약 책임보험만 가입한 경우 
보상 한도를 넘는 피해는 자신이 부담해야 합니
다. 종합보험에 가입하면 상대방과 그 차량에 대
한 추가적인 보상이 가능하고, 자기 신체와 차
량에 대해서도 보상이 가능합니다. 또한 나한테 
사고를 낸 상대방이 보험에 들지 않은 경우에도 
종합보험에 가입되어 있으면 어느 정도 보상받
을 수 있습니다.

마련하는 것이 중요합니다. 퇴직 후 수입이 없
을 것에 대비하여 가입하는 대표적인 보험상품
은 연금보험입니다. 연금보험은 국민연금과 같
은 공적연금이나 퇴직연금 외에 부족한 연금액

연금보험은 노후 보장을 위해 가입하는 상품인 
만큼 보험가입 기간이 깁니다. 중간에 해지할 경
우 납입한 보험료 중 일부를 돌려받지 못할 수

(3) [Automobile Insurance] 
Insurance to Compensate for 
Damage Caused by Automobile 
Accidents
Automobile insurance provides protection 
against personal and material damage caused 
by automobile accidents. There are two types of 
automobile insurance: liability insurance, which 
is compulsory automobile insurance, and com-
posite insurance, which drivers can purchase 
optionally. 
Liability insurance guarantees the compensa-
tion within limits for the victims and the vehicle 
damaged by the accident. If you purchase only 
liability insurance, you must bear the costs of the 
damages exceeding the limit of compensation 
covered by the liability insurance. Composite in-
surance covers damages incurred to the victims 
and his/her vehicle as well as your bodily inju-
ries and vehicles. Also, even if the person who 
caused the accident does not have insurance, 
your composite insurance compensates your 
damages to some extent.
(4) [Pension Insurance] Insurance 
that Supports Post-Retirement 
Life
As average life expectancy continues to rise, 
financial security after retirement is assuming 
more importance than ever before. One com-
mon post-retirement insurance protection is a 
pension plan. Individuals voluntarily sign up for 
this plan to supplement pension payment from 
the National Pension and Severance Payment 
System.
Because pension insurance aims to provide 
income support to retirees, the duration of the 
enrollment period before payment begins can 
be long. It is important to join a plan that is af-
fordable so that you do not end up terminating 
the policy before retirement and losing the pre-
miums you have already paid.

question: I'm currently using my account at Shinhan Bank to pay my utility bills via the automated machine, but I keep thinking about switching to my external bank account to save on monthly transfers. The guide says internal transfers are free while external ones cost a fee, but sometimes fees are waived if I meet certain transaction requirements. If I pay my utility bill at a machine located in a bank that is NOT my primary bank, will I be charged a fee for that specific transaction? Also, does the 'fee waiver' for meeting transaction requirements apply to bill payments made at ATMs in other banks, or is that only for online transfers between accounts?
answer:
Based on the provided text, here is the step-by-step reasoning for your situation:

1. **Fee for Bill Payment at Non-Primary Bank:** The guide explicitly states under 'Paying through a Special Bill-paying Machine' that if you use a machine at a bank other than where you hold your account (your primary bank), 'you may have to pay a fee.' Therefore, using a Shinhan Bank machine with an account from a different bank will likely result in a transaction fee for that specific utility payment.

2. **Fee Waiver Application:** The text mentions that fee waivers for inter-bank transactions usually depend on satisfying specific 'requirements for bank transactions' (such as transaction volume or balance), specifically in the context of 'sending money' (transfers) via internet banking. It does not explicitly state that these same waiver criteria apply to physical bill payments made at third-party bank machines. The rule for machine payments is separate: 'If you go to a bank other than your primary bank, you may have to pay a fee. So, if you want to save the fee, [use your own bank's machine].' 

3. **Conclusion:** You will likely be charged a fee for paying your utility bill at a machine in a bank where you do not hold an account. The general 'transaction requirement' waivers mentioned are contextually tied to money transfers and do not automatically guarantee a fee exemption for bill payments processed at foreign bank ATMs. To avoid the fee, you should use the automated machine at your primary bank.

your account to another account in the same

Your internet banking service will be terminated

and change the passwords in person to use the 
service again. 
When sending money to another person using

Visit your bank - Visit your bank with your bankbook (or card) and the 
utility bill (“Giro”).
은행 방문 - 통장이나 카드, 공과금 지로통지서를 준비하고 은행을 방문합니다.
Insert the Giro bill into the machine - Press [Payment], insert the Giro 
bill, and press [Insert Complete].
공과금 수납기에 넣기 - 지로납부를 누른 후 지로통지서만 투입구에 넣고 투입완
료를 누릅니다.
Take the receipt and bankbook - Keep the receipt as your proof of pay-
ment.
통장이나 카드 및 영수증 챙기기 - 통장이나 카드를 받고 인쇄되는 영수증을 확
인합니다.
Tear the Giro bill off the customer receipt - Separate the Giro notice 
and keep the customer receipt.
지로통지서와 고객용 영수증 분리 - 지로통지서와 고객용 영수증을 분리하고 고
객용 영수증은 보관합니다.
Pay with bankbook or card - Select the bankbook or the bank card you 
want to use and press your 4-digit PIN.
통장이나 카드로 결제 - 통장이나 카드를 넣고 비밀번호 4자리를 눌러 결제합니다.
④ Paying through a Special Bill-paying  
Machine
At most banks, you can make payments for 
utility bills using a special bill-paying machine. 
You can use your bankbook or payment card to 
pay your bills through this machine. If you go to 
a bank other than your primary bank, you may 
have to pay a fee. So, if you want to save the fee 
for this machine, visit your primary bank.

question: I'm a PhD candidate (D-2-4) from Vietnam currently working as a research assistant at a designated 'Specific Research Institution' under the Special Act on the Cultivation of Specific Research Institutes. My university is inviting me for this D-2-5 research process. The handbook says that for this specific D-2-5 case, I can be exempt from submitting the standard bank deposit proof and financial guarantee. However, I also saw that generally, all D-2 visa holders need to prove financial capability for living expenses. Since I'm already in Korea and switching from D-2-4 to D-2-5 while remaining in the same university, do I absolutely need to submit the bank statement showing 1 year of living expenses, or does the 'Specific Research Institution' exemption override the general financial requirement entirely? I need to know if I can skip the bank documents completely or if I need a different type of proof like a stipend confirmation.
answer:
Based on the regulations provided, here is the step-by-step reasoning to resolve your situation:

1. **Identify Your Status**: You are a PhD candidate (D-2-4) transitioning to a research process student (D-2-5) while being invited by a 'Specific Research Institution' under the Special Act.

2. **Analyze the General Rule**: Page 6 states that generally, international students must submit proof of financial capability, specifically a bank deposit covering living expenses for one year.

3. **Analyze the Exception Clause**: Page 6 explicitly lists an exception for 'Specific Research Institutes' (designated under Article 3 of the Special Act). It states that for students invited by such institutions for the D-2-5 research process, the standard requirements for 'proof of financial capability' (like the bank statement) and 'attestation of solvency' can be replaced or waived.

4. **Determine the Alternative Proof**: The document specifies that for this specific exception, you do not need the standard bank deposit. Instead, acceptable proof includes a 'Stipend Payment Confirmation' or similar documents proving research funding.

5. **Conclusion**: Since your university is a designated 'Specific Research Institute' and you are being invited for the D-2-5 process, the general rule requiring a 1-year bank deposit is overridden. You are **exempt** from submitting the standard bank statement. However, you are not exempt from proving financial capability entirely; you must substitute the bank statement with a **Stipend Payment Confirmation** or a letter confirming your research funding.

**Final Answer**: You do not need the standard bank deposit proof, but you must submit a Stipend Payment Confirmation or research funding proof instead.

question: I'm a 24-year-old international student from Vietnam currently in my final year at BUFS as a Bachelor's degree candidate. I have a TOPIK Level 3 certificate and have worked part-time legally for 4 years during my studies. I'm really confused about the Point System Job Search Visa (D-10-1) requirements. Based on the scoring table, I know I need at least 60 total points and a minimum of 20 points in the 'Basic' category to qualify. Can you break down my specific situation? I'm getting 10 points for age (since I'm 24), 15 for my Bachelor's degree, and 10 for my 4 years of work experience. Does this meet the 20-point 'Basic' threshold, and do I have enough total points to apply for the D-10-1 visa right after graduation?
answer: Let's calculate your eligibility step-by-step based on the D-10-1 Point System criteria. First, we need to check the 'Basic' category threshold, which requires at least 20 points. Looking at your profile: your age (24) falls in the 20-24 range, earning you 10 points. Your final education is a Bachelor's degree, which earns 15 points. Your 4 years of work experience (which falls in the 3-4 year range) adds 10 points. Summing these up for the 'Basic' category: 10 (age) + 15 (degree) + 10 (work) = 35 points. Since 35 is greater than the required 20, you successfully meet the Basic category requirement. Next, we calculate your total score to see if it reaches the 60-point minimum. Your current sum is 35 points. You also have a TOPIK Level 3 certificate. According to the table, TOPIK Level 3 (or 3rd stage) awards 10 points. Adding this to your previous total: 35 + 10 = 45 points. Unfortunately, your current total of 45 points is below the required 60 points. Therefore, with your current profile, you do not yet qualify for the D-10-1 visa application because you are short by 15 points, despite meeting the Basic category minimum.

한국, 특히 부산외국어대학교는 제 유학의 로망이었습니다. 
세계적인 학교로, 외국인 유학생을 위한 다양한 프로그램을 
운영하며 유학생이 한국 생활에 빨리 적응할 수 있도록 돕는 
시스템이 잘 갖춰져 있습니다. 
입학 당시 제 한국어 실력은 부족했지만, 탄뎀 프로그램에 
세 차례 참가하면서 원어민에게 한국어를 배우고, 동시에 
제가 인니어를 가르치면서 실력을 키울 수 있었습니다. 일반 
수업과 달리, 탄뎀에서는 구어체 한국어와 실제 생활에서 
사용되는 표현을 많이 배울 수 있었고, 덕분에 한국어 회화가 
훨씬 수월해졌습니다. 또한 이 프로그램을 통해 한국인 친구
들을 사귈 수 있었고, 유학 생활 중 친구를 사귀는 가장 좋은 
방법이라고 생각합니다. 이러한 경험 덕분에 제 세계는 더욱 
넓어졌습니다. 
부산외국어대학교에는 PSC 프로그램이라는 독특한 기회도 
있습니다. 저는 교수님의 추천을 받아 한국 학생 두 명과 함께 
해외 현장 학습을 진행하는 전공 융합 글로컬 프로젝트에 
참여했습니다. 2023년 하계 방학 동안 13박 14일 일정으로 
발리와 자카르타를 방문하며, 인도네시아에서 활동하는 한국 
사업가들을 만나 귀중한 지식을 얻을 수 있었습니다. 이 경험
은 창업에 대한 새로운 시각을 열어 주었고, 학교에서 활동비
까지 지원해 주었기에 더욱 값진 기회였습니다. 유학생이
라면 꼭 도전해 보기를 추천하고 싶습니다.

Pay Your Credit Card Bills on Time, so as not to Pay a Late Payment Fee
You can use your credit card to buy something even when you don’t have enough money for 
it. But if you don’t manage your credit card spending well, you may not be able to pay your 
credit card debt on time. In such cases, your credit card company will demand a higher inter-
est rate in the future and the record of overdue payment would adversely affect your credit 
at other financial institutions. So, you should be well informed when issuing credit card. For 
some people, using a check card instead of a credit card may be a good option to spend within 
a preset budget. In addition, using one or two credit cards is also recommended so that you 
do not have to keep track of too many credit cards.
Adjust the Credit Card Usage Limit.
If your credit card limit is higher than the appropriate cost of living, you may be under finan-
cial difficulties due to overspending. It is wise to adjust the card limit considering your income 
and circumstances.

신용카드 이용한도를 조정합니다.
신용카드 이용한도가 적정 생활비보다 높은 경우 과소비로 경제적 어려움에 처할 수 있습니다. 
본인의 소득과 형편에 맞게 카드 이용한도를 잘 조정하여 사용하는 것이 현명합니다.

신용카드도 체크카드와 마찬가지로 분실 시 해

로부터 피해를 보상받을 수 있습니다. 
한편, 급히 돈이 필요할 때 신용카드회사로부터 
정해진 금액 내에서 ATM이나 전화로 간편하게 
대출(현금서비스, 카드론)을 받을 수도 있습니
다. 다만, 일반적인 대출보다 금리가 높고 자주 
이용할 경우 신용도에 나쁜 영향을 주기 때문에 
이용하기 전에 꼭 필요한 돈인지 신중히 생각하
는 것이 좋습니다.
Unlike check cards, you can also buy something 
and make payments in installment with a credit 
card. Installment payments may be an attractive 
option when you do not have enough money

too large to pay all at once. However, you should 
remember that fees which are higher than that

If you lose your credit card, you should report it 
right away to your credit card company as you

receive it because your signature will help you 
be compensated for damages by the credit card 
company. 
If you have your own credit card, you can also

remember that interest rates on cash advances

whether you really need the money before using

question: I just arrived from Uzbekistan with a D-2 visa three weeks ago and haven't finished my Alien Registration yet because the office is backed up. My home country has a private health plan, but I'm confused about the timeline. Do I need to buy the university's short-term group insurance right now even though I have that foreign coverage, or does having insurance from home exempt me from the mandatory 1-3 month private insurance requirement before NHIS kicks in? I don't want to pay twice, but I also can't risk being uninsured during this gap.
answer: Based on the guidelines, here is the reasoning for your situation: First, the policy states that National Health Insurance (NHIS) enrollment is only automatic after you complete your foreigner registration, which can take 1 to 3 months. During this specific gap period, private insurance is mandatory for comprehensive coverage. However, the rules explicitly differentiate between students with and without home-country coverage. The text notes that students who already possess insurance from their home country are NOT required to purchase additional insurance in Korea. Therefore, because you have a valid plan from Uzbekistan, you fall under the exception. You do not need to buy the university's short-term group insurance or a separate personal policy during this waiting period, provided your home coverage remains active. You will still need to enroll in NHIS once your Alien Registration is finalized, but the immediate requirement to buy a *new* Korean private plan is waived due to your existing foreign coverage.

Insurance Guide for 
BUFS International Students
1. Eligible Applicants: Students who have completed Alien Registration
 ※ RC registration may take time a few weeks to several months after your application.
1) International students with a D-2 visa or overseas Koreans are required to subscribe to the 
National Health Insurance Service (NHIS) starting March 1, 2021. (The subscription date may vary 
depending on the visa type.)
2) o ensure comprehensive coverage, all PNU international students must hold both NHIS and 
private insurance (either PNU Group Insurance or personal insurance.
3) Failure to pay contributions will result in restrictions on your visa.
※ In accordance with the 'Act on the Protection of Personal Information,' all inquiries related to 
NHIS must be made directly with NHIS to ensure the protection of personal information.
 (NHIS) T. 033-811-2000 ① ENG/KOR ② CHN ③ VNM ④ UZB
*Note: The National Health Insurance information provided above has been summarized for 
students' convenience and is subject to change. For the most accurate and up-to-date details, 
please visit the NHIS website or contact their customer service center directly.
2. Types of Insurance
It takes 1–3 months after arrival to complete foreigner registration and enroll in NHIS. During this 
period, private insurance is mandatory unless you already have coverage from your home country. 
After registration, NHIS enrollment is automatic, and private insurance becomes optional.
A. Private Insurance (Univ. Group Insurance or Private Insurance)
1) Types of Insurance
- Students with insurance from their home country are not required to purchase additional 
insurance in Korea.
※ However, students may choose to have double coverage if desired.
- Students without insurance from their home country must purchase short-term insurance in Korea.

① Sending Remittance Online
To send money online, customers should access 
the bank’s website through a computer or tablet 
PC and process overseas remittances on their 
own. When you use internet banking services, 
you can save the remittance fees of up to 50%.

Remittance does not take place right after you complete your remittance transaction 
at the bank. In fact, it takes up to three business days to complete the money transfer 
depending on the country to which you send the money.
Remittance is delivered to the desired recipient only when all of the information 
you have provided such as account number of recipient, transaction bank, branch 
name and name of country is checked as correct. So, make sure you provide correct 
information to avoid any unexpected delay or failure of your remittance resulting 
from the incorrect information.

question: I just arrived from Vietnam as a self-funded D-4-1 language trainee, and I've been warned about voice phishing scams because my banking knowledge in Korea isn't great yet. I know that government-sponsored scholars from certain countries (like Egypt or Peru) get a visa that allows me to work part-time under specific 'Special Activities' rules, but I'm not on that list. My friend who is a certified foreign tour guide on an E-7 visa says he can work legally if his company has a good track record of bringing in tourists. Since I only have a D-4-1 visa for language study and I'm not a government scholar, am I legally allowed to take a part-time job as a tour guide to cover my living costs if a travel agency hires me? Or does my visa type strictly prohibit any paid work regardless of the employer's qualifications?
answer: Let's break this down based on your specific situation. First, looking at your visa status: you are a self-funded D-4-1 (language training) student from Vietnam. The regulations state that while government-sponsored scholars from 21 specific countries might have different visa issuance conditions, self-funded students generally fall under strict limitations. Second, let's look at the job you are interested in: working as a tour guide. The document regarding Professional Activities (E-5) and Specific Activities (E-7) explicitly states that tour guides must be on an E-7 visa, which requires a recommendation from the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism and proof that the employment agency has a strong track record of hosting international tourists (at least 200 foreign tourists annually). Crucially, the D-4-1 visa is designated for 'language training' and does not grant the automatic right to engage in paid employment, unlike the E-7 visa which is specifically designed for professional service roles. Even though your friend has a valid E-7 visa and a qualified employer, your D-4-1 status does not have a provision to convert to or perform E-7 activities just because the employer is qualified. Therefore, you cannot legally work as a tour guide on a D-4-1 visa. Engaging in such work would violate your visa conditions. Your priority right now should be focusing on your studies and using the provided financial fraud prevention resources to protect your personal assets, rather than seeking unauthorized employment.

question: I'm a student from Uzbekistan on a D-2 visa who plans to go back home for six months to help my family, but I'm worried about my re-entry status. Also, I've been paying into a long-term life insurance policy for two years, and if I stop paying premiums now to save money while I'm away, will I get any of that money back? I need to know: since my absence is less than a year, do I still need to apply for re-entry permission, and how does cancelling my insurance now specifically affect my financial safety if I can't pay during the study break?
answer:
Let's break this down step-by-step based on your situation. First, regarding your visa and re-entry: According to the 2025 BUFS International Student Roadmap (Page 13), students with a D-2 visa who are registered foreigners are exempt from applying for re-entry permission if their return is within 'one year'. Since you are only away for six months, you do NOT need to apply for re-entry permission before leaving. You can simply use your Alien Registration Card to return. However, upon your return, you must ensure your visa and registration are still valid or reactivate them if necessary.

Second, regarding your insurance: Page 65 of the insurance guide explains that most life insurance products have long contract periods. It explicitly states that if you terminate or stop paying premiums before the contract expires, especially for long-term products, you 'will not get back the premiums you have been paying' or only receive a portion, depending on the specific terms. Unlike car insurance which is short-term, stopping payments now for a long-term policy means you risk losing the financial safety net you built over two years. Therefore, while you don't need a re-entry permit, cancelling the insurance now is financially risky and likely yields no refund, leaving you unprotected during your time abroad.

Q &A

1. 보험에 대해 알아보기
(1) 보험이란?
보험은 사망, 질병, 장애, 실직, 노후 등 어려움을 
당했을 때를 대비하여 미리 준비하는 것입니다. 
보험은 보장받고자 하는 사항에 대해 일정액의 
보험료를 납입하면 사고가 발생했을 때 보험금
을 지급받는 것으로, 쉽게 생각하면 여러 사람들
이 공동으로 돈을 모아 사고를 당한 사람에게 지
급하는 것과 유사합니다. 갑작스럽게 사고나 질
병이 발생했을 때 보험금을 받으면 많은 도움이 
됩니다.
보험상품은 가입기간이 긴 상품이 많습니다. 자
동차보험의 경우 가입기간이 1년으로 짧지만, 
대부분의 생명보험 상품은 보험료를 내는 기간
이 깁니다. 특히 보험료를 내는 기간이 긴 상품
은 보험료 납입을 중간에 그만두게 되면 그동안 
납부했던 보험료 일부 또는 전부를 돌려받지 못
할 수 있기 때문에 본인의 재정 상태를 잘 파악
하고 가입해야 합니다.
How to Use Insurance Services
보험 이용하기
Ⅳ
1. About Insurance 
(1) What are Insurance Services?
We subscribe to various kinds of insurance in 
preparation for unexpected difficulties such as 
death, illness, disability, unemployment, or old 
age. Insurance is to pay a certain amount of pre-
miums for the items you want to be insured for, 
and you will be paid the insurance money in the 
event of an accident. In plain words, insurance 
works by pooling money or premium from many 
and providing benefits to those who qualify due 
to the occurrence of accident. It is very helpful 
to receive insurance money in case of a sudden 
accident or illness.
Many insurance products have a long contract 
period during which you have to pay premiums. 
In the case of automobile insurance, the con-
tract term is as short as one year, but most life 
insurances have a long period to pay premiums. 
In particular, for products with a long premium 
payment period, if you cancel or terminate your 
insurance contract before it expires, you will not 
get back the premiums you have been paying. 
So, make sure you learn and understand the 
terms and conditions of your insurance policy 
before you buy or cancel one. 
(2) Common Types of Insurance
Insurance is generally divided into two types: 
social insurance, and private insurance. Social 
insurance is offered by the government to cover 
the social risks that can happen to the public. In 
Korea, there are four types of social insurances, 
namely: the National Health Insurance; Employ-
ment Insurance; National Pension; and Industrial 
(2) 보험의 종류
보험은 크게 사회보험과 민영보험으로 나뉩니
다. 사회보험은 국민에게 발생할 수 있는 사회적 
위험을 보험을 통하여 대처할 수 있도록 국가가 
운영하는 제도입니다. 한국에는 4대 보험이라고 
일컫는 국민건강보험, 고용보험, 국민연금, 산업
재해보상보험 등의 사회보험이 있으며, 이 외에
IV. How to Use Insurance Services
78

(3) [자동차보험] 자동차 사고로 인한 
피해를 보상하기 위한 보험 
 
자동차보험은 자동차 사고로 인한 인적, 물적 피
해에 대비하기 위한 보험입니다. 자동차보험은 
자동차를 구입하면 의무적으로 가입하는 책임보
험과 운전자가 임의로 가입할 수 있는 종합보험
이 있습니다. 
책임보험에 가입하면 사고를 냈을 때 피해를 입
은 상대방과 그 차량에 대해 한도 내에서 보상을 
받을 수 있습니다. 만약 책임보험만 가입한 경우 
보상 한도를 넘는 피해는 자신이 부담해야 합니
다. 종합보험에 가입하면 상대방과 그 차량에 대
한 추가적인 보상이 가능하고, 자기 신체와 차
량에 대해서도 보상이 가능합니다. 또한 나한테 
사고를 낸 상대방이 보험에 들지 않은 경우에도 
종합보험에 가입되어 있으면 어느 정도 보상받
을 수 있습니다. 
(4) [연금보험] 노후생활을 보장하기 
위한 보험 
평균 수명이 길어지고 있으므로 퇴직 후에도 안
정적인 노후생활을 영위할 수 있도록 대비책을 
마련하는 것이 중요합니다. 퇴직 후 수입이 없
을 것에 대비하여 가입하는 대표적인 보험상품
은 연금보험입니다. 연금보험은 국민연금과 같
은 공적연금이나 퇴직연금 외에 부족한 연금액
을 보충하기 위해 개인이 임의로 가입하는 상품
입니다. 
연금보험은 노후 보장을 위해 가입하는 상품인 
만큼 보험가입 기간이 깁니다. 중간에 해지할 경
우 납입한 보험료 중 일부를 돌려받지 못할 수 
있으므로 본인의 소득액을 고려하여 납입금액을 
신중하게 선택해야 합니다.
(3) [Automobile Insurance] 
Insurance to Compensate for 
Damage Caused by Automobile 
Accidents
Automobile insurance provides protection 
against personal and material damage caused 
by automobile accidents. There are two types of 
automobile insurance: liability insurance, which 
is compulsory automobile insurance, and com-
posite insurance, which drivers can purchase 
optionally. 
Liability insurance guarantees the compensa-
tion within limits for the victims and the vehicle 
damaged by the accident. If you purchase only 
liability insurance, you must bear the costs of the 
damages exceeding the limit of compensation 
covered by the liability insurance. Composite in-
surance covers damages incurred to the victims 
and his/her vehicle as well as your bodily inju-
ries and vehicles. Also, even if the person who 
caused the accident does not have insurance, 
your composite insurance compensates your 
damages to some extent.
(4) [Pension Insurance] Insurance 
that Supports Post-Retirement 
Life
As average life expectancy continues to rise, 
financial security after retirement is assuming 
more importance than ever before. One com-
mon post-retirement insurance protection is a 
pension plan. Individuals voluntarily sign up for 
this plan to supplement pension payment from 
the National Pension and Severance Payment 
System.
Because pension insurance aims to provide 
income support to retirees, the duration of the 
enrollment period before payment begins can 
be long. It is important to join a plan that is af-
fordable so that you do not end up terminating 
the policy before retirement and losing the pre-
miums you have already paid.
IV. How to Use Insurance Services
90

명목으로 대포통장으로 돈을 송금받아 이를 가

③ 보증료, 전산비용 등 각종 비용이 필요하다며

④ 휴대전화, 통장사본, 체크카드 등을

대출을 위해 통장사본, 체크카드, 휴대전화가 필
요하다면서 보내달라고 요구합니다. 본인 명의
3. Loan Fraud
These scammers present themselves as employ-
ees from well-known financial companies and 
pretend that they offer loans. Their promotion 
channels include flyers, phone calls and text 
messages without face-to-face contact. They ask 
you to pay for their loan brokerage service and 
disappear once they receive your payment.
(1) Loan Fraud and Some Common 
Characteristics
① Scammers send text messages randomly.
Scammers randomly send messages saying 
that they provide low interest rate loans. If you 
call and ask about a low interest rate loan, they 
demand your personal information such as your 
phone number.
② Scammers impersonate a bank or the 
government to offer loan with lower 
interest without condition, etc.
Scammers deceive people by saying they will 
convert high-interest rate loans to a low-interest 
rate loan or a government-supported loan for 
the ordinary people, and they swindle money 
by receiving money using the illegally acquired 
bank account in the name of repayment fee of 
high-interest loan.
③ Scammers demand payment for guaran-
tee fee and data processing fee.
Scammers ask you to pay a guarantee fee, saying 
that most foreign residents’ credit score is usually 
not good enough for a loan. They claim the fee is 
needed to guarantee a loan. They also say they 
need to collect data processing fee to delete a 
bad credit history.
④ Scammers ask you to send personal 
information.
Scammers ask you to send your phone number, 
a photo copy of your bankbook and your check 
card numbers to get a loan. Your bankbook and

question: I am an international student from Vietnam working as a specialist in a top 500 company abroad for the past 18 months before coming here. I just found out that my major at the university is completely unrelated to this previous job. I know there are two ways to get a quota exemption: one for high-income earners (3x GNI) and another for specialists in 'special free zones' with government recommendation. Since I don't earn 3x GNI yet, and I don't live in a special free zone, is there any other logical way my 18 months of experience at a top 500 firm could count towards a work visa exemption despite my major mismatch? Or is my only option to wait until I graduate and apply under the standard 'related field' requirement?
answer:
Let's break this down based on the regulations provided. First, the 'Special Free Zone' route requires a recommendation from a specific government official (Mayor/Governor) and applies to those working within designated zones, which doesn't seem to fit your description. Second, the 'High-Income Specialist' route explicitly requires an annual salary of at least 3 times the per capita Gross National Income (GNI), which you stated you don't meet yet. 

However, looking at the 'General Requirements' section for introducing new occupations (Page 42), there is a specific clause for 'Talent Utilization' type occupations. It states that if a candidate has worked as a specialist for over one year at a top 500 global company, the standard academic degree and major-related experience requirements can be waived or treated differently due to the 'necessity of employment' being recognized. 

The text mentions that for candidates with a 'Top 500 company' background (1 year+ experience), the requirement for 'related field' experience can be waived if the employment necessity is acknowledged. Since you have 18 months (which is > 1 year) of experience at a top 500 firm, your lack of a related major does not automatically disqualify you from this specific exemption category. The key is not your major, but the prestige and duration of your past employment. Therefore, you should not necessarily wait until graduation. You can apply under the 'Talent Utilization' exception using your Top 500 company history as the primary justification to bypass the major mismatch, provided the employer can verify the 'necessity of employment' for your specific role.

question: I'm a first-year international student from Vietnam arriving next week. I already bought a private travel insurance policy in Vietnam that covers my entire stay, but I heard students must also have domestic short-term insurance if they don't have the national health insurance yet. Do I need to buy the separate Korean short-term insurance from sos911.co.kr as well, or is my Vietnamese policy enough to satisfy the requirement before I get my alien registration card? Also, if I do need to buy the Korean one, do I have to cancel my home country policy?
answer:
Based on the guidelines, let's break this down. First, the rule states that every international student must have valid insurance from the moment of arrival until they are automatically enrolled in the National Health Insurance (which usually happens about 2 months after classes start, once the alien registration is complete). 

Second, regarding your specific situation: The manual explicitly states that if you have purchased insurance overseas (like your Vietnamese policy), you are NOT required to purchase the domestic short-term insurance in Korea, provided you submit proof of your overseas coverage to the International Student Support team. It mentions that while you can choose to buy both if you wish, it is not mandatory to hold two policies.

Therefore, you do not need to buy the Korean short-term insurance from sos911.co.kr. Your Vietnamese policy is sufficient as long as you submit the insurance certificate to the support team immediately upon arrival. You also do not need to cancel your home country policy; you can keep it for the duration of your studies.

question: I'm currently a master's student here, but I just realized I'm eligible for a specific visa extension as a 'ship operating essential professional' because I have a license to work as a navigation officer on a Korean-flagged passenger liner, and I plan to work on the Yeonggeum-san tour ship for 6 months while finishing my thesis. The regulations say 'ship operating essential professionals' can get a single-entry visa for 1 year or less based on the consular officer's discretion, but my academic status is 'Domestic Professional Junior College or higher degree holder'. I'm confused: since I have a master's degree (which is higher than the 'Professional Junior College' threshold mentioned for general skilled workers), do I automatically qualify for the 1-year discretion visa as a 'ship essential professional', or does my academic degree force me into a different visa category that requires a 1-year employment contract first? Basically, does the 'essential professional' override the standard degree requirement, or do I need to prove the employer's need separately regardless of my master's status?
answer:
To resolve your situation, we need to analyze the intersection of your academic credentials and your specific employment role. First, according to the regulations on page 95, having a 'Domestic Professional Junior College or higher degree' (which includes your Master's degree) makes you eligible for certain visa categories. However, your specific case falls under the special provision for 'Ships' listed on page 38. The text explicitly states that 'ship operating essential professionals' (such as captains, engineers, and navigation officers on Korean-flagged regular passenger ships like the Yeonggeum-san tour ship) are eligible for a single-entry visa with a stay of 1 year or less, granted at the 'consular officer's discretion'.

The reasoning for your eligibility is as follows:
1. Your role as a navigation officer on a Korean-flagged passenger liner fits the exact definition of 'ship operating essential professional' provided in the text.
2. The regulation for this specific role (Page 38, Item 2) is a distinct category that grants a short-term visa (under 1 year) based on the nature of the work, separate from general employment visas that might strictly require a 1-year contract.
3. While your Master's degree (Page 95) qualifies you for higher-level skilled visas, the specific clause for 'ship essential professionals' acts as a specialized pathway. It does not negate your degree; rather, your degree likely strengthens your 'qualification proof' (one of the required documents: degree/certificate copy).

Conclusion: You do not need to prove a 1-year employment contract in the traditional sense because the regulation for 'ship essential professionals' explicitly allows for a '1 year or less' single-entry visa based on the consular officer's discretion. Your Master's degree serves as valid proof of qualifications but does not force you into a different category; instead, it supports your application under the 'ship essential professional' clause. You should apply as a 'ship essential professional' and submit your license, degree, and a letter of employment demonstrating the necessity of your role on the specific vessel.

Wrap-up    정리하기
1. Check the type of government voucher and eligibility.
Foreign residents may be eligible for financial assistance for hospital expenses related to prenatal 
care, delivery, and post-natal care if they are registered as a member or a dependent of a member 
of the national health insurance plan. Since the benefits and application methods are different for 
each service provider, please visit the Community Service Center (Community center) in your juris-
diction or access the “Find a voucher that suits you” on the National Happiness Card website (www.
voucher.go.kr).
1. 국가바우처 서비스 종류 및 이용자격을 확인합니다.
외국인의 경우에도 국민건강보험 가입자나 피부양자로 등록되어 있으면 임신·출산에 관련된 진료비용 등 다양
한 지원을 받을 수 있습니다. 서비스 제공기관별 혜택과 신청 방법이 다르므로 주소지 행정복지센터(주민센터)
에 방문하여 확인하거나, 국민행복카드 홈페이지(www.voucher.go.kr)의 “내게 맞는 바우처 찾기”를 통해 확인
해보시기 바랍니다
2. You can apply for the National Happiness Card.
Since you can use the government voucher only with a card, you must first receive the National 
Happiness Card. You can apply for issuance at the branches of a nearby bank, post office, or credit 
card companies (B.C, Lotte, KB Kookmin, Shinhan Cards), or on the website of financial companies 
and through the call center.
2. 국민행복카드를 신청할 수 있습니다.
국가바우처 서비스 신청을 위해서는 국민행복카드를 가지고 있어야 하며, 가까운 은행, 우체국 또는 카드사(BC·
롯데·삼성·KB국민·신한카드)를 방문하거나, 각 금융회사의 홈페이지와 콜센터 등을 통해 발급이 가능합니다.
3. Use of a local card.
If you use a local card, you can enjoy a discount or cashback service from a minimum of 5% to a 
maximum of 30% of the amount you spent with the local government budget.
Please check the benefits you are entitled to with the local currency issued by the local govern-
ment on the Local Administration Integrated Information System website (www.laiis.go.kr). You 
can apply for local card by visiting a bank that has signed a contract with a local government or by 
installing an app for local currency.
3. 지역화폐카드를 활용하세요.
지역화폐카드를 이용하면 지방자치단체 예산으로 지출금액의 최소 5% ~ 최대 30%까지 할인 또는 캐시백을 지
원받을 수 있습니다.
내고장알리미(www.laiis.go.kr) 홈페이지를 통해 주소지 관할 지방자치단체에서 발행되는  지역화폐의 혜택 등
을 확인해 보시기 바랍니다. 지역화폐카드 발급은 지방자치단체와 협약된 은행을 방문하거나 지역화폐 전용 앱
(App)을 설치한 후 신청하시면 됩니다.
If you use the local card up to the monthly limit first and then use another payment card, you can reduce your

question: I'm a first-year international student from Vietnam who just enrolled in the 'Global Communication Competency' track. I want to take the English 'College English' courses (6 credits) instead of the Korean ones to save time, but I also need to fulfill the 'Writing/Reading' requirement where I'm supposed to take 'Writing that Changes Me' in the first semester and 'Writing that Changes the World' in the second. Here's my problem: The document says I can take AI-based writing courses, but it also says 'College English' students need to take a level test in February. Since I'm starting in March for the spring semester, will taking the College English courses in the spring automatically fulfill my 'Writing that Changes Me' requirement, or do I still have to separately register for the specific 1-credit 'Writing that Changes Me' course later? I'm worried about missing the credit count if the levels don't match up.
answer: Let's break this down step-by-step based on the handbook. First, the 'Global Communication Competency' section clearly states that international students can choose between Korean or English tracks for the required 6 credits. If you choose the English 'College English' track, you must take a level test in February to register. However, looking at the 'Writing/Reading and Discussion' section, it explicitly mandates a sequence: 'Writing that Changes Me' (1 credit) in the 1st semester and 'Writing that Changes the World' (1 credit) in the 2nd semester. These are listed as distinct, mandatory subjects for all first-years, separate from the global competency credits. The document mentions that AI-based writing courses are 'possible' (trial operation) but does not state they replace the mandatory 'Writing that Changes Me' course entirely without specific substitution approval. Therefore, taking 'College English' fulfills the 6-credit communication requirement, but it does NOT automatically count as the 'Writing that Changes Me' course. You must still separately register for the specific 1-credit 'Writing that Changes Me' course in your first semester to meet the graduation roadmap requirements, regardless of your English track selection.

question: I'm an international student from Brazil who currently uses a joint digital certificate for my internet banking on my laptop, and I recently bought a new Android phone to start using mobile banking. I tried to log in to the bank's app, but it says I need a certificate on the device, and I don't want to go through the whole re-issuing process if I can avoid it. Based on the guide, if I already have a joint certificate on my PC, do I need to copy it manually to the phone, or is there a way to use the existing one directly like a cloud financial certificate? I'm confused because the text mentions both copying and cloud usage, and I need to know the exact steps for my specific setup before I reset my old phone.
answer: Let's break this down based on the banking guide. First, the text distinguishes between two types of certificates: the 'joint digital certificate' (commonly used for internet banking on PCs) and the 'financial certificate' (often stored in the cloud). If you are using the standard joint certificate on your laptop, the guide explicitly states that you must copy or download this certificate to your new smartphone via the bank's Digital Certificate Center to use mobile banking; it does not transfer automatically. However, the text also notes a specific exception: if you were already using a 'financial certificate' for internet banking (which is stored in the cloud), you could use it on your smartphone without any additional copying procedures. Since you mentioned you currently have a 'joint' certificate, the logical inference is that you do not have the cloud-based financial certificate active yet. Therefore, you must manually back up or copy your joint certificate to the new phone before changing devices. If you skip this step and reset your old phone, the certificate is deleted, and you will be forced to re-issue a new one. So, for your situation, the answer is you must perform the manual copy step; you cannot use the existing PC certificate directly on the app without that transfer.

기기)를 이용해서 은행 업무를 보는 것을 모바일

뱅킹을 통해 계좌조회, 계좌이체, 공과금 납부는 
물론 예금, 적금 가입 등 대부분의 은행 업무를

보려면 스마트폰에도 인증서가 필요합니다. 
은행 홈페이지의 인증센터에 들어가면 인터넷뱅
킹에서 발급받은 공동인증서를 스마트폰에 추가

터넷뱅킹에서 금융인증서를 사용하고 있다면, 이
러한 복사 절차 없이 클라우드에 저장되어 있는

스마트폰을 교체할 때 공동인증서를  
미리 백업해 놓으세요!
모바일뱅킹을 이용하다가 스마트폰을 교체하거나 초기
화하면 공동인증서가 삭제되어 금융 이용에 곤란을 겪
게 됩니다. 미리 공동인증서를 백업(또는 복사)해 놓으
면 인증서를 재발급받지 않고 새 스마트폰에서 모바일
뱅킹을 이용할 수 있습니다. 
When changing your smartphone,  
back up the digital certificate in advance!
If you change or initialize your smartphone while us-
ing mobile banking service, the digital certificate may 
be deleted and you may not use the mobile banking 
service. You can back up (or copy) the digital certifi-
cate in advance, and use the mobile banking on a new 
smartphone without reissuing the certificate.
(5) Using Mobile Banking Services
Mobile banking refers to using banking ser-
vices on mobile devices (smart devices) such 
as smartphones or tablets. In simple words, 
it can be described as internet banking on a 
smartphone. Through mobile banking, you can 
quickly receive most banking services such as 
account-checking, money transfer, payment of 
utility bills, as well as deposits and savings ac-
count, with your smartphone.
You should sign up for internet banking and in-
stall an App created by the bank to use the mo-
bile banking services provided by the bank. And 
a certificate should also be downloaded on the 
smartphone to run the app and perform various 
banking operations.
If you access the Digital Certificate Center on a 
bank’s website, you will find detailed instruc-
tions on how to additionally download (copy) 
to your smartphone the digital certificate issued 
for internet banking. If you are using a financial 
certificate for internet banking, you can use the 
financial certificate stored in the Cloud on your 
smartphone without going through additional 
procedures. 
Handy Tips for Using Financial Services  금융생활 꿀팁 
I. How to Use Banking Services
38

Wrap-up    정리하기
1. It is convenient to use a bank.
A bank refers to a financial company that provides almost all money-related services, such as de-
posits, withdrawals, loans, money transfer, and currency exchange. You can keep your money safe 
and collect it and earn interest through the bank.
1. 은행을 이용하면 편리합니다.
은행은 입금, 출금, 대출, 송금, 환전 등 돈과 관련된 거의 모든 업무를 처리할 수 있는 금융회사입니다. 은행을 
이용하면 돈을 안전하게 보관하고, 모을 수 있고, 이자도 생기는 등 돈을 관리하는 데 좋습니다.
2. If you don’t have time to visit the bank, use ATM, internet banking, or mobile bank-
ing.
You can enjoy banking services through ATM, internet banking, and mobile banking. ATMs allow 
you to handle a variety of tasks, such as withdrawals, deposits, account-check, money transfers, 
and updating bankbook outside of bank hours. You can also handle most transactions without 
having to go to a bank branch using internet banking or mobile banking, which is convenient, en-
joying favorable commissions and interest rate.
2. 은행을 방문할 시간이 없으면 ATM, 인터넷뱅킹, 모바일뱅킹을 이용하세요.
ATM, 인터넷뱅킹, 모바일뱅킹을 통해 은행업무를 볼 수 있습니다. ATM을 이용하면 은행 영업시간 외에도 출금, 
입금, 계좌정리, 계좌이체, 통장 정리 등 다양한 업무 처리가 가능합니다. 인터넷뱅킹이나 모바일뱅킹을 이용하
면 은행 영업점에 갈 필요 없이 대부분의 은행업무를 처리할 수 있어 편리하고, 수수료나 금리 혜택도 있습니다. 
3. Even if you can’t speak Korean, you can use banking services.
Interpretation services are available in some bank branches including those in Gwanghwamun, 
and Itaewon in Seoul and Ansan areas in Gyeonggi-do where many tourists and foreign residents 
visit or live. (Please refer to ‘Reference 1’ (p.162) for a list of stores with foreigner-only desks).
In addition, most commercial banks also operate a call center with foreign language speakers for 
foreigners who can’t speak Korean. (Please refer to ‘Reference 2’ (p.169) for foreign language coun-
seling call center numbers for each bank).
3. 한국어를 못해도 은행거래를 할 수 있습니다.
서울 광화문, 이태원, 경기도 안산 등 외국인의 왕래가 잦은 지역에는 외국인 전용 데스크가 설치된 은행 점포가 
있어 편리하게 서비스를 받을 수 있습니다(외국인 전용 데스크 점포 목록은 p.162 ‘참고자료 1’ 참조 ).
또한 대부분의 은행은 한국어에 어려움이 있는 외국인을 위해 외국어 상담 콜센터를 운영하고 있습니다(은행별 
외국어 상담 콜센터 번호는 p.169 ‘참고자료 2’ 참조 ).
4. You can pay your utility bills in various ways.
When you receive a statement of utility bill (Giro), you can pay in various ways, such as internet 
banking, mobile banking, Giro homepage, automatic withdrawal service, or visiting a bank or post 
office in person. In addition to mail, you can receive a statement of utility bill by e-mail or text mes-
sage.
4. 공과금을 내는 방법은 다양합니다.
공과금 납부 통지서(지로통지서)를 받으면 인터넷뱅킹, 모바일뱅킹, 인터넷 지로, 자동이체 서비스, 은행이나 우
체국 방문 등 다양한 방법으로 납부할 수 있습니다. 공과금 납부 통지서는 우편뿐 아니라, 이메일, 문자 등으로도 
받을 수 있습니다. 
I. How to Use Banking Services
48

question: I'm a freshman from Vietnam majoring in Computer Engineering, and I just got my first semester grades back with a GPA of 1.4. I need to send money to my family for their emergency, so I'm going to an ATM to do a transfer. I'm noticing the screen asks me to select the bank, but I also saw in the academic handbook that students with a GPA under 1.5 get course registration limits for the next term. Does the fact that I'm now on academic warning mean the university will freeze my account or block me from using the ATM to send money, or are these two completely separate systems? Also, if I enter the recipient's account number without the hyphen as the ATM guide says, will that still work even though my student status is flagged as 'warning'?
answer: Let's break this down step-by-step to resolve your confusion. First, looking at the academic handbook (Page 58), the rule for students with a GPA below 1.5 is strictly about academic administration: it limits your course registration to only 2 credits for the following semester and allows a maximum of 18 credits in specific 'academic warning' courses. This policy is designed to help you focus on your studies; it does not mention any financial penalties, account freezing, or restrictions on banking activities. Second, the ATM transfer instructions (Page 14) are based on banking protocols, not student status. The requirement to enter the account number without hyphens is a technical format rule for the banking machine's software, which operates independently of the university's student portal. Therefore, your academic warning status has no logical connection to your ability to use the ATM. The systems are separate. You can proceed with the transfer by selecting the recipient bank, entering the account number without hyphens, and confirming the amount. The academic warning will only affect your ability to register for classes in the next term, not your ability to move money.

question: I'm a first-year international student in the International College (2025 cohort) and I just took the BUFS TOPIK exam, scoring exactly level 4. I've heard that if I don't have a standard public English score like TOEFL or IELTS, I can use BUFS TOPIK as a substitute for the graduation language requirement. However, the handbook also mentions that for the 2025 cohort, there's an option to take the BUFS Korean Course (4 levels) if I don't have a score. Since I already have the level 4 certificate, do I still need to enroll in and complete the 4-level Korean course to satisfy the graduation requirement, or is my level 4 certificate enough to skip the course entirely? I want to make sure I don't waste time taking a class I don't need.
answer:
Based on the 2025 graduation certification requirements for international students provided in the academic handbook, here is the step-by-step reasoning:

1. **Identify the Requirement:** For the 2025 cohort, the graduation language requirement is TOPIK Level 4 or higher, OR a specific public English score (TOEFL iBT 59, IELTS 5.5, etc.).
2. **Analyze the Substitution Clause:** The text states: 'In the absence of a public language score, it is possible to substitute by obtaining BUFS TOPIK Level 4 or higher OR completing the Level 4 Korean Education Course.'
3. **Evaluate Your Situation:** You have already obtained BUFS TOPIK Level 4. This score directly satisfies the primary condition ('TOPIK Level 4 or higher') listed for the cohort.
4. **Determine the Necessity of the Course:** The clause presents the 'Level 4 Korean Course' as an *alternative* substitution path specifically for those who *do not* have a qualifying score. Since you already possess the qualifying score (BUFS TOPIK Level 4), the condition for taking the course is not triggered.

Conclusion: You do not need to enroll in the 4-level Korean course. Your existing BUFS TOPIK Level 4 certificate is sufficient to meet the graduation language requirement immediately.

persona.situation: First-year student in International College (2025 cohort) confusing the alternative pathway requirements.

valid_reason: The context explicitly states that for the 2025 cohort, obtaining BUFS TOPIK Level 4 is a valid method to satisfy the graduation language requirement if a public language score is absent. Since the student already holds this certificate, the alternative path of completing the 4-level Korean course is not required.

question: I'm a D-2-7 (Master's) student from Uzbekistan currently enrolled in a regular graduate program, but my department just assigned me to a 'Service Learning' course that requires me to volunteer in a local community. I also know my university is an 'Excellent Certification University' for study abroad. Does this Service Learning activity count as 'training' that would allow me to switch my visa to D-3 (Technical Training), or do I strictly have to stay on my D-2 visa because the activity is educational and not focused on industrial technology transfer to a foreign-owned company?
answer: To determine your visa eligibility, we must compare the nature of 'Service Learning' against the strict requirements for the D-3 Technical Training visa. First, the 'Service Learning' course described in the academic handbook is an educational activity where students apply classroom knowledge to community service for character development; it is not an industrial training program. Second, the D-3 visa regulations explicitly state that eligibility is limited to individuals training at specific industrial enterprises that have directly invested abroad or export industrial facilities, and the training must be for technical skills that cannot be acquired in the trainee's home country. Since your Service Learning involves community volunteering rather than technical industrial training at a qualifying foreign-invested enterprise, it does not meet the statutory criteria for D-3 conversion. Therefore, you must remain on your D-2 visa, as the 'Excellent Certification University' status only simplifies the Certificate of Visa Issuance process for study purposes, it does not transform educational service projects into industrial technical training.

수강신청 인원 초과로 수강신청을 못 했어요. 교수님 허락을 받으면 수강신청 가능한가요? 
아닙니다. 수강신청 상한인원은 강의실 정원, 교과목 특성 등의 요인을 고려하여 설정되었기 때문에, 상한 
인원을 초과하여 신청할 수 없습니다. 또한 수강신청은 정해진 기간에 학생 본인이 직접 처리하는 것으로,  
담당 교수 또는 학과 조교가 대리로 신청할 권한이 없습니다. 
☆ 졸업상의 문제로 반드시 들어야 하는 과목이라면 국제학생지원팀 담당 선생님을 통해서 학사지원팀과  
협의 
이수 구분 변경은 어떻게 신청해야 하나요? 
[ NOMAD 인재시스템(eport.bufs.ac.kr)  →  학사 정보  →  수강  →  이수 구분 변경 신청 ]에서 직접 신청
하면 됩니다. 매 학기 4월 및 10월에 [ NOMAD 인재시스템 ]에서 결과 확인 가능합니다. 
☆ 수강신청 시 전공기본 또는 전공심화실무로 이수 구분이 표시되더라도 다른 전공인 경우  
전공으로 인정이되지 않으므로, 이수 구분 변경 신청 기간 중 이수 구분 변경 신청을 필히  
본인이 해야 합니다. 
교과목 코드만 다르면 다른 과목이니까 들어도 괜찮은가요? 
그렇지 않습니다. 교과목 코드가 달라도 동일과목으로 지정되어 있으면 중복수강이 
불가능합니다. 또한 교과목명이 동일한 경우도 동일과목으로 간주되어 학점이 인정되
지 않으니 유의하시기 바랍니다. 
☆ 대체 동일과목 조회 방법 :  
[ 학생포털시스템  →  수업  →  대체 동일과목 조회 ]

valid_reason: The answer accurately synthesizes information from the provided context: Page 52 defines 'Service Learning' as an educational activity for character development, while Page 10 defines 'D-3 Technical Training' as requiring training at specific foreign-invested or export-oriented industrial enterprises. question: I am a Vietnamese student currently in the final year of my Bachelor's degree at BUFS, majoring in Computer Science. I have just submitted my thesis and expect to graduate within the next two months. I want to stay in Korea to start my own tech company immediately after graduation. I found information about the 'Startup Visa' (D-10-2) which requires a patent or being part of the 'Global Startup Immigration Center' curriculum, but I also read about the 'Job Seeker Visa' (D-10-1) which is based on a points system requiring 190 points and TOPIK 4. Since I don't have a patent yet and haven't taken the specific startup training course, can I apply for the D-10-2 visa right now based on my upcoming graduation, or am I forced to apply for D-10-1 first? Also, if I apply for D-10-1, does my current student status count towards the 'within 3 years of graduation' requirement, or does that clock only start ticking once I actually receive my diploma?
answer:
Based on the regulations provided, here is the step-by-step reasoning for your situation:

1. **D-10-2 (Startup) Eligibility**: The requirements for the D-10-2 visa explicitly state that the applicant must possess a patent, utility model, or design right, OR have completed/participate in the 'Global Startup Immigration Center' (OASIS) curriculum. Merely graduating with a Bachelor's degree and having a business idea is not sufficient on its own. Since you mentioned you do not currently have a patent and have not completed the specific training course, you do not currently meet the criteria for the D-10-2 visa.

2. **D-10-1 (Job Seeker) Eligibility**: This visa is designed for graduates who need to find employment. The requirements include holding a Bachelor's (or higher) degree, having TOPIK Level 4 or higher, and scoring at least 190 points on the points system. Additionally, it applies to those who graduated within the last 3 years. 

3. **Timeline and Status Inference**: The regulations state that the 'within 3 years of graduation' condition applies to those who obtained their degree in a regular Korean university. While you are currently a student, the specific clause for D-10-1 mentions the clock starts after obtaining the degree. However, there is a specific provision for 'first-time change of status' after graduation. If you apply for the D-10 status *after* you graduate, you are eligible. If you try to apply *before* graduation, you generally cannot switch to D-10 because your primary status is still 'Student' (D-2). 

4. **Conclusion**: You cannot apply for the D-10-2 (Startup) visa immediately because you lack the specific patent or training certification required. You are not 'forced' to take D-10-1 if you can obtain the startup credentials first, but without them, D-10-1 is your only viable option for staying. You must first graduate and receive your diploma. Once you have your degree and TOPIK 4, you can apply for the D-10-1 visa. The '3-year' count begins from the date of your graduation, not from your application date while you are still a student. Therefore, your immediate plan should be to graduate, secure your degree, and then immediately apply for the D-10-1 visa while you work towards meeting the future D-10-2 criteria (like filing a patent).

Provide remittance information to the recipient 
- Inform the recipient of the sender’s name, the amount of remit-

송금 수취인에게 정보 제공

4. Financial Fraud Involving 
Illegally Obtained Bank 
Account
(1) Some Common Characteristics of 
Illegally Obtained Bank Accounts
Illegally obtained bank account refers to an ac-
count opened in the name of a third person but 
used by another person. It is used as a means of 
receiving money from victims of fraud in various 
crimes such as voice phishing because it can 
avoid tracking by the investigatory agency. Re-
cently, voice phishing scammers have collected 
and used the illegally obtained bank accounts 
(fraudulent accounts) through various fraudu-
lent means, so you should be careful to prevent 
your bank account from being unknowingly 
used by scammers for illegal acts. 
The holder of the account used for the voice 
phishing scam can be subject to various dis-
advantages, such as suspension of payment, 
restrictions on electronic financial transactions 
such as internet banking and mobile banking, 
and restrictions on opening new accounts for 
more than one year. And for a person who trans-
fers or lends his or her bank account to another 
person, such person is liable to up to five years 
imprisonment and a fine of 30 million won, and 
the person is also subject to criminal punish-
ment for fraud and aiding fraud depending on 
the level of awareness of the crime. It is illegal to 
transfer and lend check cards and cash cards as 
well as bank accounts.
< Main Types of Financial Fraud Using 
Illegally Obtained Bank Account >
① Scammers request you to lend your bank 
account, mentioning short-term high 
profit as a bait using text message or SNS
Scammers induce unspecified number of people 
to lend or transfer the bank account saying they 
will provide short-term high profit of 100,000 
won or more per day.

question: I'm a 28-year-old international student from Vietnam who just finished my 2-year associate degree here in Korea. I've also been working part-time in a logistics company for 4 years while studying. Now I want to switch to a job seeker visa (D-10-1). Based on the point system, how many points do I get for my age, my 2-year domestic degree, and my 4-year work experience combined? Also, I'm worried because I don't have a TOPIK certificate yet, but I did complete the KIIP 3rd stage. Does that count towards the total, and do I reach the 60-point threshold needed to apply?
answer: Let's break down your eligibility step-by-step based on the D-10-1 points table. First, for your age (28), you fall into the 25-29 age bracket, which awards 15 points. Second, regarding your education, you hold a domestic 2-year associate degree (professional college), which grants 15 points. Third, for your work experience, having 3-4 years of employment in Korea earns you 10 points. So far, your base score is 15 + 15 + 10 = 40 points. Now, regarding your Korean language ability: the table explicitly lists 'KIIP 3rd stage' as worth 10 points, so yes, you qualify for those points even without the TOPIK certificate. Adding that 10 points brings your total to 50 points. However, the regulation states you need a minimum of 60 points to qualify. With your current profile, you are 10 points short. You would need to either gain more work experience (to reach the 5-6 year bracket for 15 points, gaining 5 more) or achieve a higher language proficiency (like KIIP 4th stage for 15 points) to meet the 60-point requirement.

(3) 환전하는 방법 
환전은 국가별로 서로 다른 돈을 맞바꾸는 것으
로, 예를 들어 원화를 미국 달러로 바꾸거나, 미국 
달러를 원화로 맞바꾸는 것을 말합니다. 한국에
서의 환전은 보통 은행을 통해 할 수 있습니다.
외국인의 경우 외국환매입증명서, 대외지급수단
매매신고필증 등의 증빙서류가 있는 경우 그 범
위 내에서 환전할 수 있습니다. 만약 별도 증빙
서류가 없다면 1만(미국) 달러 이내에서 환전이 
가능합니다. 이때는 외국환은행이 환전 내역을 
여권에 표시해야 하므로 반드시 환전 시 여권을 
지참하여야 합니다.

(3) How Do I Exchange Money?
Changing or converting money refers to chang-
ing money to a currency of different countries. 
For example, it means converting Korean won 
into US dollars or exchanging US dollars with 
Korean won. Currency in Korea can usually be 
converted at a bank.
Foreigners can exchange money within the 
scope indicated in the proof of purchase, such 
as a certificate of purchased foreign currency or 
declaration of means of foreign payment. If you 
have no other documentary evidence, you can 
exchange money up to 10,000 (US) dollars. In 
this case, the foreign exchange bank must indi-
cate the exchange details on your passport, so 
be sure to bring your passport when exchanging 
money.

install malicious programs (apps) or make phone

Ms. A received a text message from a friend that said,

As she clicked on the card, a malicious application 
was automatically downloaded and installed on her 
smartphone. A total of 260,000 won was paid through 
the application.

1. 저축상품에 대해 알아보기
언제 필요할지도 모를 돈을 저축한다는 것이 쉬
운 일은 아닙니다. 가장 좋은 방법은 저축 목표
를 세운 후, 매달 저축할 돈을 미리 빼놓고 돈을 
사용하는 것입니다. 아무래도 쓰고 남은 돈을 저
축하게 되면 매달 일정 금액 이상 저축하는 것이 
쉽지 않기 때문입니다. 언제든지 자유롭게 입금
이나 출금을 할 수 있는 은행의 보통예금은 이자
가 매우 낮기 때문에 목돈을 모으려면 정기적금
이나 정기예금을 적절히 활용하는 것이 좋습니
다. 또한, 은행마다 결혼이민자에게 높은 금리를 
제공하는 저축상품을 판매하는 경우가 있으므로 
가족관계증명서 및 혼인관계증명서 등 필요서류
를 가지고 은행에서 상담을 받아 보고, 본인에게 
유리한 저축상품에 가입하는 것이 좋습니다.
How to Save Money
저축상품 이용하기
Ⅱ
저는 필리핀에서 살다가 15년 전 국제결혼을 통해 한국
으로 왔습니다. 남편과 농사를 지으며 살고 있었는데, 
남편이 뇌출혈로 쓰러져 병원에 입원하게 되었습니다. 
중학생 아들과 초등학생 딸이 있어 공장에 다니며 혼자
서 살림을 꾸려오고 있는데, 얼마 전 유방암에 걸리고 
말았습니다. 남편 병원비와 생활비도 빠듯한데, 제 수술
비에 치료비까지 더해져 생활이 너무 어려워졌습니다. 
다행히 지역사회에서 많이 도와주고 있지만, 생활이 어
려워도 일찍부터 저축을 해오거나 보험에 들어두지 않
은 게 정말 후회가 됩니다.
▶ 예기치 못한 상황에 대비하여 소득의 일정 부분은 저
축을 하는 것이 좋습니다. 전문가들은 소득의 20~30% 
정도를 저축할 것을 조언하고 있습니다.
I came to Korea 15 years ago from the Philippines 
through an international marriage. I was living on a 
farm with my husband, but my husband suffered from 
a brain hemorrhage and was hospitalized. I have a son 
in middle school and a daughter in elementary school. 
I work at a factory to make a living by myself, but I 
recently got breast cancer. I live on a strict budget due 
to my husband’s hospital bills and living expenses, 
but my surgery and treatment costs have made the 
life become tighter. Fortunately, I am supported by 
the local community a lot, but I regret not having a 
health insurance or a savings account even if the cost 
of living was tight.
▶ It is a good idea to save a portion of your income 
for unforeseen circumstances. May experts say that it 
is best to save about 20 to 30 percent of your income. 
Financial Wisdom to Learn from Actual Cases  사례로 배우는 금융지혜 
1. About Saving Money
Saving money for unexpected events is not easy. 
The best way to save money is to make a savings 
plan and then save a portion of what you earn 
before spending it. This is because it is not easy 
to save a certain amount of money every month 
if you save the remaining money after spending. 
Since a free savings account which allows you to 
easily deposit and withdraw money at any time 
provides lower interest, it is recommended to 
use a fixed installment savings or fixed deposit 
savings appropriately to save a large amount 
of money. In addition, since some banks sell 
savings products that offer high interest rates to 
marriage immigrants, it is recommended to visit 
a bank with necessary documents such as family 
relationship certificate and marriage certificate, 
consult with the bank staff and sign up for a sav-
ings product that provides preference.

이 같은 수수료를 아끼기 위해 간혹 사설 브로커
를 통해 해외에 있는 가족에게 송금하는 경우가 
있는데, 중간에 돈을 가로채어 사라지는 사고가 
많이 발생하고 있으므로 특히 주의하여야 합니다.
(1) 은행 창구를 이용한 해외송금
해외 송금 방법을 잘 알지 못할 경우 은행 창구
를 방문하여 은행 직원에게 직접 안내를 받으며 
송금업무를 처리하는 것이 안전합니다. 하지만 
다른 송금 방식에 비해 수수료가 비싸고 은행 영
업시간에만 이용할 수 있다는 단점이 있습니다.
일반적으로 은행 창구에서 해외로 송금할 경
우 수수료는 금액에 따라 다르지만, 건당 최소 
5,000원~최대 20,000원 내외이며 이외 전신료
(건당 약 8,000원 수준), 해외중개 수수료, 해외 
현지 은행 수수료 등을 부담해야 합니다.
(2) 은행의 인터넷뱅킹과 모바일뱅킹을 
이용한 해외송금
은행을 방문하는 번거로움 없이 인터넷뱅킹 또
는 모바일뱅킹을 이용하여 해외송금을 할 수 있
습니다. 인터넷뱅킹 또는 모바일뱅킹을 이용하
여 해외송금서비스를 이용하기 위해서는 신분증
(여권, 외국인등록증, 주민등록증)을 가지고 거
래하고자 하는 은행 영업점을 방문해야 합니다.
은행에 가면 먼저 번호표를 뽑고 순서를 기다린 
후 본인 차례가 오면 직원에게 인터넷뱅킹 또는 
모바일뱅킹을 이용하여 해외송금을 하고자 한
다고 말합니다. 이후 직원의 안내에 따라 필요한 
사전업무를 처리하시면 됩니다.
Some foreigners residing in Korea send money 
through unauthorized or illegal brokers to save 
the remittance fee and fall victim to fraud or 
scam. You should be noted not to use the unau-
thorized brokers. 
(1) Sending Money via a Bank Teller
If you do not know how to send money abroad, 
you would be better advised to visit a bank tell-
er, and receive direct instructions from the bank 
teller for transfer. However, the fee is higher than 
other remittance methods, and in such cases, 
you may send the money only during the bank 
hours.
The amount of remittance fee usually depends 
on the amount of money you send at a bank. 
Typically, the amount ranges from 5,000 to 
20,000 won per transaction, and the cable 
charge is about 8,000 won per transaction. Bro-
kerage fee and service fee to be paid to the re-
cipient’s bank are charged differently depending 
on the bank. 
(2) Sending Money through Internet 
Banking and Mobile Banking
You can remit money abroad using internet 
banking or mobile banking without bothering 
to visit a bank. In order to use the overseas remit-
tance service through internet banking or mo-
bile banking, you must visit the branch of your 
trading bank with your ID card (passport, alien 
registration card, resident registration card).
At a bank, you first take a ticket, wait for your 
turn, and when your number is called, tell the 
staff that you want to send money overseas us-
ing internet banking or mobile banking. Then, 
you can follow the staff’s instructions to com-
plete the necessary preparatory work required 
for internet banking and mobile banking.
V. How to Exchange and Send Money Abroad

IV. 학교생활 안내

(4) ATM 해외송금 서비스
ATM을 이용하여 해외로 송금할 수도 있습니다. 
다만 사전에 ATM을 통한 해외송금이 신청이 되
어있어야 합니다. ATM에서 송금을 하려면 ‘최근 
송금내역 조회’ 후 송금하거나, 미리 영업점을 방
문하여 ‘수취인에 대한 해외송금 정보’를 등록한 
후에 하면 됩니다. 
(4) Sending Money via an ATM
You can also send money abroad using an ATM. 
To do so, you need to apply for ATM remittance 
service in advance. To send money via an ATM, 
you can check the most recent remittance trans-
action or pre-register ‘the recipient’s information’ 
at your bank.
How to Send Money Abroad via an ATM    ATM 해외송금 방법
Visit a bank - Visit a bank and register the recipient’s bank infor-
mation in advance.
은행 방문 - 외국에 송금하려는 은행 계좌정보를 은행에 방문하여 미
리 등록합니다.
Check the recipient’s account - Verify the recipient’s account 
number.
수취인 계좌 확인 - 수취인 계좌가 맞는지 확인합니다.
Print a receipt - Keep your receipt as proof of your remittance.
완료 후 영수증 출력- 영수증은 송금내역의 증빙자료가 되므로 잘 보
관합니다.
Choose the pre-registered information or the recent overseas 
remittance record - Select the recipient by checking either the 
pre-registered information or the most recent remittance activity.
등록 내역을 선택하거나 최근 해외송금 내역 선택 - ATM에서 미리 
등록한 해외송금 내역을 선택하거나 최근 송금한 송금내역을 선택합
니다.
Select the desired currency and the amount of remittance 
- Select the desired currency (Korean won or foreign currency) 
and enter the remittance amount.
화폐 종류 및 송금금액 선택 - 송금하려는 화폐 종류(원화 또는 외화)
를 선택하고 송금금액을 입력합니다.

③ 공동인증서와 금융인증서
은행을 방문해 출금할 때는 신분증을 보여주고 
도장이나 서명으로 자신이 통장 주인임을 증명
합니다. 마찬가지로, 인터넷뱅킹을 할 때도 거래
하는 사람이 본인이 맞는지를 증명하기 위해 전
자 증명서가 필요합니다. 
2020년까지는 ‘공인인증서’가 유일한 전자 증명
서였지만, 지금은 ‘공동인증서’와 ‘금융인증서’가 
그 자리를 대신하고 있습니다. 공동인증서는 과
거의 공인인증서가 이름을 바꾼 것으로 보면 됩
니다. 두 가지 인증서 가운데 원하는 것 하나를 
선택해서 발급받으면 됩니다. 먼저 공동인증서
를 발급받기 위한 절차를 자세하게 알아봅시다. 
핸드폰 문자서비스(SMS)  
또는 은행 앱을 활용한  
입출금 알림서비스를 신청하세요!
입출금 사실을 알려주는 핸드폰 문자서비스를 신청하
면 돈이 통장으로 들어오거나 통장에서 나갈 때마다 문
자로 받아볼 수 있어 편리합니다. 또한 금융사기 등 예
상하지 못한 금융거래가 발생할 경우 신속하게 은행 또
는 경찰서(☎112)에 신고해서 대응할 수 있습니다. 금융
회사를 방문하거나 은행 홈페이지 또는 은행 앱(APP)을 
이용해 서비스를 신청할 수 있습니다. 은행 앱을 활용한 
입출금 알림서비스도 가능합니다. 수수료가 부과되는 
문자서비스(SMS)와는 달리, 은행 앱을 활용한 입출금 
알림서비스에는 수수료가 없습니다.
Sign up for your bank’s text message 
(SNS) service or App Push Service using 
bank App!
When you sign up for your bank’s text message ser-
vice, you can easily check money coming in and out of 
your account through the text message. Moreover, if 
something unexpected happens - for example, some-
one trying to steal money from your account - the text 
message service can help you report it immediately to 
the bank and the police. (The telephone number for 
the police is ☎112). You may sign up for the service 
by visiting a financial company or accessing the bank 
website or bank app (APP). You may also use App 
Push Services. Unlike text messages (SMS) that charge 
a fee, App Push Service does not charge any fee.
Digital Certificate  공동인증서 로그인 화면
③ Digital Certificate and Financial Certificate
In order to withdraw money at a bank, you have 
to show your ID card and verify your identity 
as an account holder by sealing or signing on a 
document. Similarly, to use internet banking ser-
vice, you need an electronic certificate to verify 
your identity for a transaction.
Until 2020, the “Personal digital certificate” was 
the only electronic certificate, but now you can 
use a “digital certificate” or “financial certificate” 
for internet banking. Digital certificate is the new 
name of the personal digital certificate and you 
just need to select one of the two certificates to 
be issued with one. First, let’s take a look at how 
to receive a certificate, step by step.
Handy Tips for Using Financial Services  금융생활 꿀팁
